license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.notice: Event descriptions are paraphrased summaries written for this dataset. Source links point to authoritative references.

entries.title: Application Error
entries.details: Generated when an application crashes. Key fields: FaultingApplicationName, FaultingModuleName, ExceptionCode, and FaultingModuleBaseAddress. ExceptionCode 0xc0000005 (access violation) is the most common crash type; 0xc000001d (illegal instruction) may indicate shellcode or ROP chain execution. A faulting module name of UNKNOWN or an offset far outside a known module's address range can indicate exploitation. Correlate with 1001 for WER bucket data.

entries.title: Windows Error Reporting
entries.details: Generated by Windows Error Reporting following a crash (1000) or hang (1002). Contains the fault BucketId and problem signature fields (P1–P8) which identify the crash type and faulting component. BucketId can be used to correlate identical crashes across multiple systems. FaultBucketType distinguishes crash bucket types. Pairs with 1000 or 1002 — often logged a few seconds after the triggering event.

entries.title: Application Hang
entries.details: Generated when an application stops responding. HangType field indicates whether the hang was detected by Windows (ghost window timeout) or triggered by the user terminating the process. A process hanging after code injection or payload execution may indicate a stuck or partially executed implant. Correlate with 1001 for WER data and 1000 if the hung process subsequently crashes.

entries.title: .NET Runtime Unhandled Exception
entries.details: Generated when a .NET application throws an unhandled exception and terminates. The event message contains the full exception type, message, and managed stack trace. Useful for detecting failed .NET-based attacks — watch for exception types such as System.BadImageFormatException (malformed assembly load), System.Security.SecurityException (permission block), or unusual namespaces in the stack trace that do not correspond to known installed software.

entries.title: Windows Installer: Installation Operation
entries.details: Generated when a Windows Installer installation operation occurs. Records ProductName, ProductVersion, and result code. Provides early visibility into install activity before the final outcome (11707/11708) is known. Pivot on ProductName for unexpected software and the result code for silent failures. Correlate with concurrent Security log events to identify the installing account.

entries.title: Windows Installer: Removal Operation
entries.details: Generated when a Windows Installer removal operation occurs. Monitor closely for removal of security tools, EDR agents, AV products, or monitoring software — uninstalling defensive tooling is a common defense evasion technique. Pairs with 11724 for confirmation of successful removal. Correlate with concurrent Security log 4688 events to identify the process that initiated the removal.

entries.title: Windows Installer: Installation Completed Successfully
entries.details: Generated when a Windows Installer product installation completes successfully. Final confirmation of a successful install. Monitor for unexpected software installations, especially outside approved change windows or on systems that should not have new software added. The ProductName field identifies the installed product. Correlate with 1033 for the initiation event and Security log 4688 for the installing process.

entries.title: Windows Installer: Installation Failed
entries.details: Generated when a Windows Installer product installation fails. Can indicate a tampered or unsigned package, insufficient privileges, AppLocker/WDAC blocking, or a corrupted installer. Repeated failures for the same product may indicate an attacker attempting to install a blocked or flagged payload. Correlate with 1033 for the originating install attempt and Security log for privilege context.

entries.title: Windows Installer: Removal Completed Successfully
entries.details: Generated when a Windows Installer product removal completes successfully. High-priority alert when the removed product is a security tool (AV, EDR, HIPS, logging agent). Correlate with 1034 for the initiating removal event and concurrent Security log events to identify the account and process that performed the removal.
